video.title: INEOS Grenadiers CLEAN SWEEP the Podium | Volta a Catalunya Stage 7 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel into highlights of stage seven of the voltron catalonia 2021 the last stage ineos pretty much had gc wrapped up they had adam yates miles ahead of anyone else that wasn't on his own team and you can have gc movement on this stage in barcelona 135 kilometers long five eight kilometer hilly laps of a circuit around the castle of manjuith and this climbs pretty nasty it's got a 500 meter pinch in it and there has been gc movement here in the past surely with grant thomas in third 14 seconds ahead of val verde and fourth 10 seconds bonus seconds available on the line for the stage winner surely i thought mother star would ride all out for val verde they hadn't got anything out of this race yet so getting a stage win and or getting him to third position on gc he's won this stage and gc here in the past i thought that would be their play but instead they let a really large break go up the road and didn't really pace it back until the last 30 kilometers it had maurice multiple mother style riders that included mark soler and they let it get about five minutes plus as they entered this final circuit with 50ks to go and that's a pretty big margin to bring back particularly when you've got half your domestics in the breakaway and this is the break not the peloton eventually breakaway specialist thomas de kent and mattei maheret who's been in just about every breakaway this week on every stage in catalonia broke free maurice the last we'll ever see of him super tucking because it's banned in a couple of days he dropped thomas the kent on every single descent off that climb but then he waited up every time only cohen bowman for jumbo visma chased really out of the breakaway the remnants of it behind them he only got within a minute of the maharich the peloton led by motherstar now in the last couple of laps they paced with henrik mass on all of the climbs they were over three minutes behind and yates karapas and ineos didn't look under any pressure at all on the climbs and so on the final ascent of the monument climb thomas the kent hit mohric with a big attack and maurice went out of sight out of cameras pretty quickly and the hint was riding away with three k's i think to go at this point and a descent into the finish even maharaj super tucking wasn't going to be able to bring thomas to kent back now should maharich when he gave to hint on the last ascent of the climb with one more ascent to do should he have sat up for to hint at the bottom probably not he probably should have paced a little bit harder made it harder for thomas to hint he kind of made it easy for him because he was always going to drop maurice or put a bit of a gap onto him in the last climb i think both of them knew that but the problem for morich was con bowman was 50 seconds to a minute behind so he kind of needed to hemp to help him on that circuit to stay away in a gc group as they crested the final climb nothing had really happened mother star had tried a little bit simon yates countered valverde marking him killed him and still looking good ineos had their three riders on gc nicely positioned yates port and grain thomas in a group with mike woods i had mike woods for top two on this stage and i did my preview but i didn't expect him to be so close on gc which meant he couldn't go on a break today or get any leash from the gc contenders but thomas the kent what a great catalonia for lotto sudah book ending it with two wins first with andreas crone in the first stage with that late breakaway taking the leaders jersey second with thomas de gent his fifth volterra catalonia stage win a fantastic week for them and disappointment once again for marriage although a lot of exposure for bahrain victorious as well as his shape looking really good going into the ardennes classics where he can be a big threat in the gc group the last kilometer was pretty emblematic of how this week has gone garen thomas pacing on the front just like he done and richie porte had done in the last mountain stage on stage four no one really able to do anything it's this fast downhill finish there's the 10 seconds the bonus seconds are not available anymore for valverde so barring thomas having a mechanical or a puncture right now gc was already locked up and despite this not being a great week for mother star it is reassuring for them the level of valverde showed in the mountains particularly on stage three was super impressive i think they should have committed to valverde even more actually i know he's 41 years old but he's still their best rider thomas degen laughing at the gc group thinking i already finished this stage i'll enjoy my phantom thanks very much in the sun in barcelona volta took his first world tour stage podium third his teammate reichenbach fourth valverde fifth finishing off this race which has just been ineos domination in terms of the general classification borah have to be happy with two stage wins from kemner and peter segan as well as kelderman looking pretty good on gc but adam yates winning his first world tour level stage race gc in europe he won the ua tour last year 45 seconds out of teammate richie port and 49 seconds ahead of geraint thomas inios 1 2 3 on the podium that's never happened in a race of this level world tour level in the 21st century even back in the day when they had peak froome thomas port while polls they never had a one two three on gc and the big encouragement for ineos has to be adam yates his first tt on the new setup in the uae is pretty gotta be honest he lost over 30 seconds in a 13k itt to teddy pagacha and almeida and that really made it difficult for him in the later stages in the mountains he had to be on the offensive on jabella feet and he couldn't get rid of pagacha but then his second tt admittedly on a course that did suit him a little bit more here more roley maybe a little bit longer he did a fantastic effort in the time trial in catalonia on stage two only losing seven seconds to almeida and when we saw that we knew it was big trouble for almeida especially richie port as well only lost six seconds so then in the next mountain stage he didn't even need to be that aggressive but he had the good legs attacked anyway with valverde and coos was able to drop kus off the wheel and put big time into every other gc contender in this race taking out the stage win as well as grant thomas showing some of that 2018 climbing level with a last kilometer surge that must be very encouraging for ineos to see and then ineos made full use of that snack team kamna getting three and a half minutes up the road whilst being close on gc on stage four bit of a fly in the ointment no matter luke rowe castroviejo dennis mowed him down and they kept it all together on that mountain top finish to poor dna only letting chavez up the road who wasn't a threat on gc then that day when there was a 40 man split up the road they paced again with roy castroviejo dennis and kara pass kept it all together and what an advantage for them to have the giro champion richard carapan so i thought was a big threat in this race and the tt cost him to have him policing the other climbers in the mountains and bringing back moves like he did on stage four eating into henrik mass and esteban chavez gap is invaluable and i think ineos should take the bones of this team to the tour de france adam yates they've got him pencil down for the welter espana he's the only rider we've seen recently who can make pagatya sweat on a mountaintop finish he showed that in jabel her feet in the uae tour yes his consistency in three week races has been a problem previously but he's moved to the best team in the world now and arguably the most professional and i think his abilities complement thomas port and cara paz very nicely comment down below let me know what you think do you think adam yates should be taken to the tour de france by ineos i think it's a no-brainer but maybe you have a different view i hope you enjoyed these vulture catalonia highlight videos i really enjoyed making them and watching the race particularly enjoyable race but otherwise we've got the classics coming up soon check out the lantern recycling podcast for them and i'll see you later ciao you
